context: task#151 flat-CRF sweep reported codec-gs-mixed at production CRF=28 hitting 16.26 / 14.49 / 11.16 dB valid-cam mean render-PSNR on bonsai / bicycle / stump. The agent caveat noted orbit-camera coverage was uneven (1-2 of 5 cams hit non-empty content). This task disambiguates whether (a) the harness was placing cameras in empty space or (b) codec-gs-mixed genuinely hit the same render-PSNR regime as the MesonGS++ K=256 that we already killed in task#141.

root_cause_harness_bug.bug: Camera position computed as [cx + r*cos, cy + height, cz + r*sin] where caller passes `height = centroid[1] + 0.3*radius`. Net camera y = cy + (cy + 0.3*r) = 2*cy + 0.3*r — the centroid y-coordinate is added twice, lifting the orbit ring well above the scene. For scenes with non-zero centroid.y, most cameras fire into empty space (src_mean ≈ 0, PSNR pegged at 100 dB by the MSE clamp). This contaminated every prior render-PSNR gate that pulled orbit_view_mats from oracle_pipeline (including #141 MesonGS++ measurement and #151 codec-gs-mixed sweep).
root_cause_harness_bug.note_141_was_largely_ok: task#141 used a SECOND independent script (tasks/scripts/meson_attribute_isolation.py) with its OWN orbit_view_mats that does NOT have the double-cy bug. That's why #141 measured bonsai at 21.55 dB on 5 content-bearing cams. The bug only affected scripts that pulled orbit_view_mats from oracle_pipeline directly — which includes tasks/scripts/meson_render_psnr.py and the #151 codec-gs sweep harness.
root_cause_harness_bug.fix: Replaced the harness with tasks/scripts/codec-gs-sanity/psnr_v2.py — 8 cameras around the opacity-weighted centroid with pitch jitter, look-at toward centroid, plus a built-in src-vs-src baseline assertion. Bad-baseline + empty-cam flags are emitted alongside content-cam rows so future agents can't be fooled by the same artifact.

harness_v2.camera_strategy: 8 cams around opacity-weighted centroid at radius = 1.5 * IQR-extent, pitches [-15°, 0°, +15°, 0°, -15°, 0°, +15°, 0°], all look_at(centroid).
harness_v2.baseline_check: PSNR(src,src) rendered twice — must read >= 99 dB on every cam. Cams that fail are flagged BAD_BASELINE and excluded from the content-cam mean.
harness_v2.content_filter: src_mean > 0.05 (threshold 2.5× higher than #151's 0.02). Cams below threshold are flagged EMPTY and excluded.
harness_v2.result_layout: per_cam_flag + per_cam_src_mean + per_cam_det_baseline_db + per_cam_psnr_db + content_cams + content_mean_psnr_db + content_min_psnr_db

disambiguation_matrix.note: All cells have src-vs-src baseline = 100 dB on every cam (8/8) and src_mean >= 0.15 on every cam. New cameras are content-bearing across all 3 scenes.

bonsai_iter7000.note: Same order of magnitude. v141 used a sibling script (meson_attribute_isolation.py) that doesn't have the orbit_view_mats bug. v2 harness hits stricter cameras and reads 2.7 dB lower; both numbers confirm MesonGS++ K=256 is in the ≤20 dB regime.

bicycle_iter7000.note: First independent measurement of MesonGS++ K=256 bicycle. Confirms MesonGS++ kill was justified.

verdict_rationale: All three scenes hit ≤20 dB content-cam mean at production CRF=28. Decision rule for ≤20 dB: real Principle 2 violation, remove from picker.

codec-gs-mixed is unambiguously worse than MesonGS++ K=256: 12.7 dB on bicycle vs MesonGS++'s 14.8 dB on the same scene + cam set. We already killed MesonGS++ — keeping codec-gs-mixed in the picker is inconsistent with the precedent.

All 8 cameras are content-bearing (src_mean 0.15-0.74) so the result is not an artifact of empty-space cameras. src-vs-src baseline = 100 dB on every cam proves the gsplat render is deterministic and the codec is the real error source.

Caveat: MesonGS++ on bonsai dropped from 21.55 dB (v141 cams) to 18.89 dB (v2 cams). v2 cams are harder. The decision rule (≤20 dB → remove) was set against v1-cam numbers; applying the same threshold to v2 cams is stricter than originally intended. However, even under the v3 'marginal' band (20-30 dB), codec-gs-mixed wouldn't qualify — bonsai 16.8 dB, bicycle 12.7 dB, stump 13.9 dB are all clearly below 20 dB.
actions_taken: Added new render-PSNR harness tasks/scripts/codec-gs-sanity/psnr_v2.py with content-cam placement + baseline check; pinned as the canonical Principle 2 test set for future gates.
Removed `codec-gs-mixed` entry from apps/web/src/components/TryIt.astro presetOptions with an inline kill-comment citing this memo.
Left apps/api/src/pricing.rs entries for `codec-gs-mixed` / `codec-gs-mixed-k5` intact (same #141 pattern — research can still drive the codec via API).
Left apps/worker/worker.py + crates/splatforge-cli/src/main.rs dispatch routes intact (research-only access).

Documented the oracle_pipeline.orbit_view_mats double-cy bug in this memo so it's traceable; the harness used in #141 (meson_attribute_isolation.py) was independently correct, so MesonGS++ kill stands as-is.
